# SRC006 - The Effectiveness Code

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Advertising Effectiveness |
| Authors / source | WARC / Cannes Lions / Kantar partnership |
| Year | 2020 |
| Source type | Large effectiveness-awards analysis |
| Quality | Medium |
| URL | [https://www.warc.com/en/article/the-effectiveness-code-63d75d4dfc3547f4b8a84f6f4d1bf6e2](https://www.warc.com/en/article/the-effectiveness-code-63d75d4dfc3547f4b8a84f6f4d1bf6e2) |

## Bias / limitation

Award-entry bias; still a valuable creative-effectiveness map.

## Core takeaway

Creative commitment and strategic consistency matter; not all creativity is equal.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use to design a creative platform, not just isolated variants.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.

## Research extraction template

```markdown
### Source extraction

- Source ID: SRC006
- What it changes about our strategy:
- What it proves:
- What it does not prove:
- Ad tactic suggested:
- Landing page proof suggested:
- Measurement implication:
- Follow-up source needed:
```
